# Saved Sets Checklist: UC-28 Save and Name Constraint Sets

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, and consistency of requirements for saving, naming, overwriting, renaming, deleting, and recovering named constraint sets
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/028-save-constraint-sets/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for creating a named constraint set from the current term-scoped constraint configuration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are storage-scope requirements explicit for both student identity and registration term boundaries? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK003 Are all saved-set management actions in scope for this feature explicitly documented, including save, overwrite, rename, delete, and failure handling? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define whether reuse means listing only, applying the set, or both? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for the zero-state scenario where no configured constraints exist before save is attempted? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is the saved-set naming rule fully quantified, including trimming, maximum length, and allowed characters? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK007 Is the duplicate-name comparison rule explicit enough to determine exactly when two names conflict? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK008 Is the overwrite policy defined with an unambiguous trigger and decision point for the student? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK009 Is the term “full current constraint set” specific enough to determine what data must be captured at save time? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK010 Is “current unsaved constraints available for correction” defined clearly enough to know what state must remain visible after a rejected save? [Clarity, Spec §US2]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the save-failure requirements consistent between user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §US3, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK012 Do the duplicate-name edge case and overwrite requirements align without conflicting outcomes? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK013 Are rename requirements consistent with duplicate-name validation and overwrite policy rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK014 Do the term-scoping requirements align across save, list, rename, delete, and reuse flows? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-008]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can each success criterion be objectively verified without inferring hidden implementation behavior? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK016 Are the acceptance scenarios sufficient to distinguish rejection, overwrite confirmation, and successful overwrite as separate outcomes? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US2]
- [x] CHK017 Is the rename/delete success criterion measurable enough to verify both list updates and storage consistency after reload? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary, alternate, exception, and recovery scenarios all represented in the requirements for named set management? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for stale-session conflicts during rename, overwrite, and delete actions, not just save? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK020 Does the spec define what happens when a student attempts to rename a set using its own current normalized name? [Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are boundary conditions for naming, duplicate normalization, empty-state save attempts, and cross-term isolation all addressed in requirements? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are failure-handling requirements defined for each mutating action, including rename and delete, not only the initial save path? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK023 Are rollback expectations explicit when a mutating action fails after user confirmation? [Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK024 Are performance requirements quantified for all mutating actions and not just the initial save path? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-001]
- [x] CHK025 Are plain-language messaging requirements specific enough to cover overwrite prompts, delete confirmations, invalid names, and stale-session refresh instructions? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-002]
- [x] CHK026 Are keyboard-accessibility requirements defined for every saved-set management action and modal decision point? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-003]
- [x] CHK027 Are privacy and ownership requirements explicit for viewing, listing, renaming, overwriting, deleting, and reusing saved sets? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-004]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are assumptions about existing constraint-editing functionality and saved-set reuse behavior validated against the stated feature scope? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK029 Are dependencies on profile storage and saved-set list reload behavior documented clearly enough to support acceptance validation? [Dependency, Spec §Dependencies]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK030 Does the spec avoid ambiguous terms such as “reuse,” “current,” and “available” unless each term is operationally defined in context? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK031 Is a maximum number of saved sets intentionally excluded, or is it a missing business-rule requirement carried over from the use case open issues? [Gap]

## Notes

- This checklist assumes standard reviewer depth for PR or spec review.
- Focus areas selected: naming-policy clarity, saved-set lifecycle coverage, and recovery/conflict handling.
- Actor/timing: reviewer during pre-implementation requirements review.
- Checklist review closed by tightening reuse semantics, saved-state visibility after rejection, same-name rename handling, full rollback requirements, stale-session scope, and the explicit out-of-scope note for maximum saved-set count.
